title: Forn Dodonna
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Dodonna}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:Forn_Dodonna_Render.jpg]] |name=Forn Dodonna |homeworld=[[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Gray |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|This is Admiral Forn Dodonna to the ''Ebon Hawk'', do you read us?|Forn Dodonna|}} '''Forn Dodonna''' was an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. She was one of the most senior of naval personnel during the war. Towards the end of the war, Dodonna was signaled by [[Carth Onasi]], who informed her of the [[Star Forge]], a massive factory-space station that was being used by the Sith. Dodonna led the assault on the Star Forge, with the help of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vandar Tokare]]. Helped by the battle meditation of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] Bastila Shan, Dodonna broke through the Sith blockade of the Star Forge and ordered her ships to destroy the space station's main stabilizer. She later awarded the crew of the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', including Carth, for their help during the battle. ==Biography== {{Quote|Hit that Star Forge with everything we've got!|Forn Dodonna during the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]]|}} As an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] serving with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Republic Navy/Legends|Navy]], Admiral Dodonna led Republic forces against the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]] from her [[Admiral Dodonna's flagship|flagship]]. Towards the end of the war, Admiral Dodonna was contacted by another naval officer, [[Carth Onasi]], informing her of the location of a massive orbital factory called the [[Star Forge]], an ancient relic of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]]. The Star Forge was being used to churn out all the starships and war droids that powered the Sith fleet. Dodonna realized that this was the opportunity she desperately needed to put an end to the war. Gathering a fleet together, Admiral Dodonna set out for the [[Lehon system|Star Forge system]]. En route, Admiral Dodonna's fleet was reunited with many survivors of the Sith attack on the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. Led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vandar Tokare]], the group joined the fleet in its endeavor to destroy the Star Forge. [[File:Dodonnahologram.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Dodonna as a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]], communicating with the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''.]] Upon arriving in the system, Admiral Dodonna's fleet was confronted with a sizeable defense fleet, which guarded the Star Forge itself. Dodonna was contacted by Carth Onasi once more, from the ''Ebon Hawk'', whose crew deactivated a massive interdictor field being generated from the nearby planet [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]], which would have otherwise destroyed the fleet. Admiral Dodonna was suffering heavy losses in space and suggested a retreat, but Carth insisted that the Republic probably wouldn't get another chance to strike at the Star Forge again. Dodonna conceded, and the ''Ebon Hawk''{{'s}} crew, at the advice of Master Vandar, resolved to lead a Jedi strike team onto the Star Forge, to find and kill [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Darth Malak|Malak]], the leader of the Sith Empire. Meanwhile, Dodonna was facing troubles of her own. Her entire fleet was faced with an impenetrable defensive line, preventing her from ordering a bombardment of the Star Forge. Carth attributed this to [[Bastila Shan]], a fallen [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who was very adept at using [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], a [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Force power/Legends|ability]] that allowed her to inspire allied troops and demoralize enemies, influencing an entire battle. Bastila's battle meditation was allowing the Sith to quickly counter every strategy Admiral Dodonna devised. Nevertheless, Admiral Dodonna pressed on. [[File:Kotorships_copy.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Dodonna's [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|capital ships]] reinforce the positions of [[Red Squadron (Galactic Republic)/Legends|Red]] and [[Green Squadron (Galactic Republic)|Green Squadrons]].]] Conditions soon changed after Bastila stopped using her battle meditation against the Republic fleet. Soon Dodonna's relentless press against the Sith ranks finally punctured a breach in the fleet's formation, splitting it into two groups. Admiral Dodonna quickly exploited this development, sending [[Red Squadron (Galactic Republic)/Legends|Red Squadron]] in with [[Green Squadron (Galactic Republic)|Green Squadron]] to widen the gap. She then ordered her capital ships to reinforce their positions. [[File:Sf-battle.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Dodonna's fleet moves to attack the [[Star Forge]] directly.]] Once in range, Admiral Dodonna's fleet commenced a concentrated assault on the Star Forge. Realizing that the Star Forge was too well reinforced to be destroyed directly by the fleet, Dodonna proposed a precision strike against the Star Forge's main orbital stabilizers. With these out of commission, the Star Forge would fall out of orbit and crash into the star below. This strategy was quickly implemented. After Admiral Dodonna's capital ships destroyed the stabilizers, she ordered the fleet to pull back away from the Star Forge, unwilling to risk any of her ships being destroyed during the Star Forge's descent. The Star Forge was finished, colliding with a solar flare which caused the Star Forge to incinerate from within. With the Star Forge destroyed and Malak dead, the Sith fleet was thrown into disarray, allowing the Republic to sweep through Sith space and recover their losses. Returning to Lehon, the fleet celebrated their victory. Dodonna herself awarded the crew of the ''Ebon Hawk'' with the [[Cross of Glory]], the Republic's most prestigious medal, on the steps of the [[Temple of the Ancients]] (the very place that the crew had disabled the disruptor field generator at), thus commemorating them for their invaluable support during the [[Battle of Rakata Prime|battle]]; even the crew's droids were given medals. The remainder of the war was short; the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] homeworld of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] quickly tore each other apart as each attempted to seize the title of Dark Lord of the Sith, a title held by Malak until his death, in a quick yet brutal civil war. This was the final blow to the Sith war effort, and the Jedi Civil War was over, though the Republic still had to recover from the destruction caused by the war. It can be speculated that Dodonna had Carth promoted to admiral during this time. Admiral Onasi would play an important role during the [[Dark Wars]].''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Carth, you made it!''"
"''We couldn't let you start the victory party without us, Admiral.|Forn Dodonna and Carth Onasi|}} Admiral Dodonna was very strong-willed. After all, she was one of the few female fleet commanders in recorded history.''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' Despite her gender, she was respected by her officers, and they served with her faithfully. Dodonna was compassionate, and cared for her entire fleet, showing concern for it and considering a retreat several times during the Battle of the Star Forge. Dodonna was also appreciative of those who helped her, and she was modest enough to accept help when offered. Dodonna awarded the crew of the ''Ebon Hawk'' with the Cross of Glory, and gave them credit for the battle's outcome, not keeping any of the glory for herself. But compassion does not gain rank, and Admiral Dodonna's military traits were what afforded her the high rank she possessed. Admiral Dodonna was a consummate military commander, overseeing every step of an operation and employing a variety of substrategies to achieve victory. This multitasking and micromanaging could prove a distraction, however, and Dodonna could lose sight of a key enemy maneuver during the heat of battle. Nevertheless, this trait was crucial during the Battle of the Star Forge, when each fighter counted against Bastila's battle meditation. From the smallest fighter to the largest capital ship, Admiral Dodonna perceived them all as equally important, and used them to the best of their abilities. When she saw an opportunity, she wasted no time in seizing it. During the Battle of the Star Forge, Dodonna skillfully enlarged a small breach that had formed in the enemy's formation, pushing her capital ships in behind her fighter squadrons, effectively wedging her formations and cleaving the Sith ranks in two, allowing her to bombard the Star Forge itself. Dodonna, as a military officer, was a perfectionist. She was neatly groomed, her hair cleanly trimmed, and her uniform spotless. She had exceptional posture, notable especially due to her tall, lean build. Dodonna had brown hair and gray eyes. ==Behind the scenes== ===Dark side ending=== {{Quote|We're about to pull back. We're taking heavy losses against the Star Forge.|Forn Dodonna|}} Upon arriving in the [[Lehon system|Star Forge system]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Dodonna's fleet came in contact with a [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] defense fleet, guarding the [[Star Forge]]. Dodonna's fleet engaged the Sith fleet, taking heavy losses. She was contacted thereafter by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Bastila Shan]]. Bastila informed Admiral Dodonna that they had deactivated the disruptor field guarding the Star Forge, and also that [[Carth Onasi]] did not survive the attack on [[Temple of the Ancients|the field's generator]]. With the loss of Carth, with which Dodonna was deeply regretful of, Dodonna considered a full-scale retreat, to strike at the Star Forge at a later date. Bastila, however, strongly suggested that they press the attack. [[Vandar Tokare|Master Vandar]] also suggested that Bastila and her [[Revan/Legends|Jedi companion]] lead a strike team to find and capture [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Darth Malak|Malak]], leader of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Bastila agreed, and set out for the Star Forge. [[File:Starforgebattle2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sith fleet defends the Star Forge against Dodonna's attack.]] Soon, however, Dodonna was shaken by the fact that the Sith were able to counter every maneuver she attempted to enact, each counterstrike executed with masterful precision. Master Vandar informed Admiral Dodonna that Bastila must have fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], using her [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] to help the Sith fleet. Master Vandar suggested a retreat, but Admiral Dodonna discouraged it, saying that they would never get another chance to attack the Star Forge again, and resolved to fight to the bitter end. [[File:Sithswarm.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Sith fighter]]s swarm in effort to overwhelm Dodonna's position.]] The tide continued to turn against her, however, and a massive wave of [[Sith fighter]]s launched an attack on Admiral Dodonna's ranks. Many of [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|her own fighters]] were destroyed during this stage of the fighting. While preoccupied with the fighter attack, a [[Interdictor-class cruiser|Sith battleship]] launched a point-blank assault on her fleet, causing Dodonna to lose many of her ships. Admiral decided that there was no choice for her but to retreat. However, Master Vandar gravely pointed out to her that the Sith fleet had cut off their escape route. Realizing that her fleet was doomed, Dodonna waited for the end. A large wing of Sith fighters moved to intercept [[Admiral Dodonna's flagship|Dodonna's flagship]]. After a few minutes of concentrated fire, her shields were overloaded, and her flagship was subsequently obliterated by the Sith fighters, taking Dodonna, Vandar, and her crew with it. With Dodonna removed from the battle, the Sith easily overwhelmed the remainder of the Republic fleet. Meanwhile, an amnesiac [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]], the Sith leader before Malak, had found his true identity, killed Darth Malak, and retook the mantle of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. He and his new apprentice, Bastila herself, then began construction of a massive fleet called the Infinite Fleet, utilizing the full might of the Star Forge. With Dodonna, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] from [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], and the fleet itself gone, Darth Revan was poised to assault and quickly conquer the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. However, this fear never became reality, as Darth Revan mysteriously vanished, and the Sith fleet soon disbanded. The tatters of the fleet were hunted down by the remaining [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic forces]]. Nevertheless, Dodonna's presence would still be missed during the crucial restoration period after the war, and the concurrent [[First Jedi Purge|Jedi Purge]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Forn Dodonna}} {{Reflist|1}} {{Interlang |de=Forn Dodonna |es=Forn Dodonna |fr=Forn Dodonna |it=Forn Dodonna |pl=Forn Dodonna }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Dodonna, Forn}} [[Category:Commenori]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Republic Navy admirals]]
